qa_pairs.question: Which hand is held at mid-chest in a fist with thumb extended upward, palm inward, while the other arcs inward to end above it?
qa_pairs.options: Left hand / Right hand / Both hands

evaluation.reason: Statement 1 says the left hand is in a fist at mid-chest with palm inward while the right hand arcs to end above it

qa_pairs.question: Which hand forms an F-hand at upper-chest level and makes a small downward dip with a slight bounce while the other stays lowered?
qa_pairs.options: Left hand / Right hand / Both hands

evaluation.reason: Statement 2 specifies the right hand forms an F-hand at upper-chest level and dips, with the left hand lowered

qa_pairs.question: During the small downward dip at upper-chest level, is the palm facing upward?

evaluation.reason: Statement 2 states the palm is facing forward and slightly right during the dip, not upward

qa_pairs.question: Answer only "touch" or "not touch" - whether two hands visibly touch each other or not

evaluation.reason: Statement 3 explicitly states "touch," indicating the hands visibly touch

qa_pairs.question: Which side changes from the index–thumb circle to a crossed index-over-middle shape while moving briefly upward-forward with the palm facing forward?
qa_pairs.options: Left hand / Right hand / Both hands

evaluation.reason: Statement 2 specifies "The right hand ... changes ... palm facing forward ... moving a brief upward-forward path."

qa_pairs.question: Which side maintains the index–thumb circle at lower chest with the palm oriented inward during the other's change?
qa_pairs.options: Left hand / Right hand / Both hands

evaluation.reason: Statement 2 says "the left hand holds the index–thumb circle at lower chest with palm inward."

qa_pairs.question: At any time, are both palms facing forward simultaneously?

evaluation.reason: Statement 1 has both palms inward; Statement 2 has only the right palm facing forward while the left remains inward, so never both forward simultaneously

qa_pairs.question: Answer only "touch" or "not touch" - whether two hands visibly touch each other or not

evaluation.reason: Statement 3 explicitly states "not touch," and no touching is described in Statements 1 or 2

qa_pairs.question: Which hand performs two small taps against the cheek?
qa_pairs.options: Left hand / Right hand / Both hands

evaluation.reason: Statement 1 says the dominant right hand makes two small tapping motions against the cheek, while the left hand stays low

qa_pairs.question: Which hand maintains a vertical flat shape at upper chest level while the other moves in small arcs nearby?
qa_pairs.options: Left hand / Right hand / Both hands

evaluation.reason: Statement 2 states the non-dominant left hand holds a vertical B-hand at upper chest level while the right hand moves in small arcs nearby

qa_pairs.question: Is the palm of the cheek‑tapping hand facing outward away from the face?

evaluation.reason: Statement 1 specifies the palm of the cheek-tapping hand is facing inward toward the face, not outward

qa_pairs.question: Answer only "touch" or "not touch" - whether two hands visibly touch each other or not

evaluation.reason: Statement 2 describes movement in front of the left palm without contact, Statement 1 keeps the hands separate, and Statement 3 explicitly says "not touch"
